gaps.want: remind me in 20 minutes / in an hour to X
gaps.needs: resolveDateTime support for relative offsets ('in N minutes/hours'), or a duration slot type plus a set-reminder branch that computes now+duration

gaps.want: remind me at five / at half past six / at 8 (bare hour)
gaps.needs: number-word and bare-hour time parsing in resolveDateTime; note bare digits are deliberately excluded today as the task-vs-reminder discriminator, so only word-times are safely addable

gaps.want: remind me tomorrow to X (date, no clock time)
gaps.needs: router change: a required datetime slot with a prompt currently hard-rejects the template (router.dart line 171) instead of matching and letting the skill's isNull branch ask for the time; alternatively a date-slot template variant with a default time

gaps.want: remind me every weekday / every weekend to X
gaps.needs: recurrence DSL extension (weekly day-set, e.g. weekly:mon,tue,wed,thu,fri) plus a skill or input to emit it

gaps.want: remind me every monday and thursday at 9am
gaps.needs: multi-day weekly recurrence; _nextWeekly handles a single day name only, and 'monday and thursday' silently degrades to a one-off

gaps.want: remind me on the 15th of every month at 9am
gaps.needs: day-of-month monthly recurrence (monthly-date form); set-monthly-reminder only encodes monthly:<ordinal>:<weekday>

gaps.want: remind me every year on march 3 / annually
gaps.needs: yearly recurrence skill (birthdays are a separate subsystem)

gaps.want: what reminders do i have tomorrow / this week
gaps.needs: list-reminders has no date-filter input; needs a filtered variant skill or an optional date input

gaps.want: remind me an hour before my meeting
gaps.needs: event-relative scheduling; stays on the cloud path

gaps.want: robust handling of 'every second tuesday' (many speakers mean biweekly)
gaps.needs: policy decision or confirmation step; corpus routes it to set-monthly-reminder (Nth weekday) per spec, biweekly requires 'every other'/'every two weeks'/'fortnight' literals

gaps.want: graceful failure on unrecognized weekday text (e.g. 'tuesdays' captured raw, 'tues and thurs')
gaps.needs: weekday validation at skill time with a prompt; today reminders.dart _nextWeekly silently falls back to a one-off when the day name is unknown

gaps.want: correct date capture in 'remind me to X tomorrow at 5pm' (postfix day phrase)
gaps.needs: the existing 'remind me to {text} at {when}' template captures text='X tomorrow' and loses the day; the router does not backtrack across alternative slot splits, so this needs post-capture date extraction from the text slot or split backtracking

notes: ORDERING IS LOAD-BEARING; the router takes the first corpus entry whose regex matches AND whose typed slots resolve. Insert this additions block as one contiguous run, in the exact order given, immediately AFTER the existing set-monthly-reminder templates and BEFORE the existing set-weekly-reminder templates in corpus.json (i.e. before 'remind me every {weekday} at {when} to {text}'). Rationale: (1) trailing-recurrence forms like 'remind me to {text} every {weekday} at {when}' are regex-matchable by the existing one-off 'remind me to {text} at {when}' (text greedily absorbs 'X every tuesday'), so every recurring addition must precede the existing one-off block; (2) within the block, daily < biweekly < monthly < weekly: weekly's bare '{weekday:text}' would otherwise swallow 'day' (daily), 'other tuesday' (biweekly) and 'second tuesday' (monthly), and monthly's '{ordinal} {weekday}' would swallow 'other tuesday' if biweekly's literal 'other' didn't come first; (3) the new biweekly leading form 'every other {weekday} at {when} remind me...' must precede the EXISTING weekly leading form 'every {weekday} at {when} remind me...', which the chosen insertion point guarantees. The four set-daily-reminder templates are outside this cluster's assigned skills but are required ordering guards for the new weekly trailing forms (and useful coverage; a daily skill already exists). The one-off set-reminder additions are safe at this position because every one is anchored by a reminder literal AND datetime-gated: an unparseable when (e.g. 'every tuesday at 9am', 'tomorrow' with no clock time) rejects the template and falls through, so 'remind me {when:datetime} to {text}' cannot steal recurring or dateless utterances. Plural weekdays are handled by putting the literal s OUTSIDE the slot ('{weekday:text}s at') so the slot captures the singular that reminders.dart's _weekdays map recognizes. 'last' works as the monthly ordinal (ordinal_num maps last to -1), so 'remind me to X on the last friday of every month at 4pm' is covered by these templates. Deliberately omitted as over-match risks: '{_:text} remind me to {text} at {when}' (would match "don't remind me..."), '{_:text} all my reminders' (would match 'delete all my reminders'), bare '{text} at {when:datetime}' (no literal anchor), and 'what do i have coming up' (no reminder anchor; collides with tasks/calendar). Apostrophes in templates (don't, what's) are escaped literally by the compiler and matching is case-insensitive; if STT emits 'dont'/'whats' without apostrophes, add those variants then.
